title: Proposal Writer/Editor
description_text:
About Us
Integrity Management Services, Inc. (IntegrityM) is an award-winning, women-owned small business specializing in assisting government and commercial clients in compliance and program integrity efforts, including the prevention and detection of fraud, waste and abuse in government programs. Results are achieved through data analytics, technology solutions, audit, investigation, and medical review.
At IntegrityM, we offer a culture of opportunity, recognition, collaboration, and supporting our community. We thrive off of these fundamental elements that make IntegrityM a great place to work. Our small, flexible workplace offers an exceptional quality of life and promotes corporate-driven sustainability. We deliver creative solutions that exceed goals and foster a dynamic, idea-driven environment that nurtures our employees’ professional development. Large company perks…Small company feel! 
http://www.integritym.com
We are seeking a creative Federal Proposal Writer to join our team. The right candidate will be a strong collaborator, highly organized to help manage marketing needs, and rapidly synthesize information from disparate sources to draft compelling, persuasive content. Projects will include (but not be limited to) formal proposals, marketing collateral, blogs, whitepapers and other business development material.
Responsibilities 
Work closely with the Marketing team, Business Development Team, Executive team, subject matter experts, and other IntegrityM personnel to produce winning content
Responsible for creation, edits, and final review of proposed publications 
Will help manage marketing needs via Trello/other digital PM tools
Gather necessary information from program personnel and research applicable policy, regulations, and best practices to support the team in developing, writing, formatting, and editing technical and non‐technical information for reports, white papers, blogs, marketing collateral, procedures, processes, briefs, and other forms of documentation
Provide technical and editorial document review and written feedback on documents
Maintain regular and frequent direct communication with subordinates to clearly articulate goals and priorities, and build trust and confidence
Meet with proposal team to strategize win themes and proposal approaches
Interview technical subject matter experts and develop written narrative from these interviews
Write a variety of proposal sections, including executive summaries, technical approaches, management plans, and other content, and integrate proposal content authored by other writers into cohesive, compliant technical proposals with the flow of one voice
Proofread and edit all work product: Review all proposals for consistent style and formatting according to company style guidelines. Ensure accurate spelling, punctuation, and grammar
Stay informed of market developments in creative areas through reading, industry involvement, and research to determine competitive factors our company must address to stay viable and profitable
5+ years’ experience as a content writer/editor. Federal Proposal writing experience a strong plus.
Bachelor’s degree in English, Technical Writing, Communications; preferred
Administrative/project coordination experience preferred
Highly organized and can help manage marketing needs via Trello/other digital PM tools
Experienced in the art of persuasive writing in proposals, blogs, whitepapers, etc.
Excellent verbal communication skills including a robust vocabulary and thorough understanding of proper English language usage
Excellent listening skills including the ability to interview staff and translate information into a clear, compelling message
Excellent written communication, including proofreading, grammar, spelling and editing skills
Must be comfortable collaborating with senior staff and operating in a fast-paced environment while adapting to changing project requirements.
Strong time management habits and ability to meet deadlines
Strong visual organization skills with keen attention to detail
Self-motivated and able to work with minimal supervision
Proficient with MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint), Skilled in Internet usage and research
Knowledge of government contracting; preferred analytics and/or solutions 
Digital Marketing & Graphic Design experience is a plus
Experience with HHS & CMS 

IntegrityM is an Equal Opportunity Employer and we do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, color, sex, age, national origin, religion, sexual orientation, and gender identity, status as a veteran, and basis of disability or any other federal, state or local protected class
ai_core_responsibilities: The Proposal Writer/Editor will collaborate with various internal teams to produce winning content, including drafting, editing, and finalizing formal proposals, marketing collateral, blogs, and whitepapers. Responsibilities also involve managing marketing needs via digital PM tools and gathering necessary information through research and subject matter expert interviews.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have 5+ years of experience as a content writer/editor, with Federal Proposal writing experience being a strong plus, and preferably a Bachelor's degree in English, Technical Writing, or Communications. Essential skills include highly organized project coordination, persuasive writing, excellent verbal and written communication, and proficiency with MS Office.

linkedin_org_description:
Our goal is to empower clients to deliver on their missions, enhance program/payment integrity initiatives, and elevate best practices utilizing decades of experience in healthcare fraud, waste, and abuse (FWA). At Integrity Management Services, Inc. (IntegrityM), we strive to safeguard the integrity of healthcare systems while prioritizing the protection of our clients and beneficiaries.

IntegrityM, an award-winning Certified Women-Owned Small Business, specializes in optimizing payment and program integrity for both Federal and nonfederal programs. Our healthcare expertise spans across various domains, including Medicare (Part A, B, C, and D), Medicaid, Managed Care, and the Marketplace. With over 20 years of experience, our team excels in consistently identifying and mitigating numerous cases of Medicare and Medicaid improper payments, program vulnerabilities, and potential FWA. Our solutions include statistical and data analysis, coding and medical reviews, fraud investigations, audits, compliance reviews, grants management, training and education, and technology solutions.

For nearly two decades, IntegrityM has been a leader in program/payment integrity services, FWA prevention and detection, and compliance services. We serve a broad range of clients, including government agencies, health plans, and other healthcare providers. Our team of investigators, auditors, medical review nurses and coders, statisticians, data analysts, and Medicare and Medicaid subject matter experts converge expertise and experience providing exceptional customized solutions. We take pride in our in-depth knowledge and application of healthcare regulations, identification of the latest fraud schemes, and analysis of large datasets, combined with agility and adaptability, allowing us to rapidly meet our clients’ program needs.

title: Medical Administrative Assistant
description_text:
We are looking for a Medical Administrative Assistant to join our team! 
Are you someone who thrives in a fast-paced office environment where your time management, attention-to-detail, and communication skills are put to good use? Are you looking to switch from a hospital environment to remote office work? We might have the perfect entry/mid-level opportunity for you. Join our medically-based concierge service and early intervention program and help impact injured workers lives by coordinating services, providing resources to our constituencies, and helping people when they need it most. 
The Injury Coordinator (working title: Medical Administrative Assistant) works in a remote office setting to:
Manage and/or assign files to appropriate staff member and initiate appropriate verbal and/or written contacts with employers, clients, claimants, and medical providers. 
Set up files in all appropriate systems; assign files, when applicable, to the nurse. 
Maintain responsibility of an individual caseload.
Maintain appropriate electronic and paper files. 
Interface with a variety of inter-disciplinary providers (e.g., PT, diagnostic, psychology, etc.). 
Identify, maintain, and update participating providers. 
Answer incoming calls, and direct the call appropriately. 
Process all documents using computer, copier, and scanner. 
Search and copy the appropriate internal criteria guidelines, when appropriate.
Work as part of a team to meet turn-around time for the set activity. 
Keep supervisor advised of any problems or delays. 
Continually improve job skills and knowledge of all company products and services as well as customer issues and needs, through ongoing training and self-directed research. 
Adhere to company policies, procedures, and reporting requirements.
Experience in any of the following disciplines a plus – CNA, Medical Assistant, Physical Therapy Aide, Workers’ Compensation, Medical Unit Claims Administrator, IME Coordinator, Medical Office Manager
Experience with workers' compensation or disability (a plus!)
Bachelor's or Associate's Degree preferred but not required
Intermediate computer and internet skills (will work with programs including MS Word, Outlook, and Excel) 
Basic knowledge of/ability to read medical reports 
The ability to research evidence-based guidelines 
An understanding of workflow guidelines and follow as necessary
Proficient verbal/telephone and written communication skills 
A high level of efficiency, ability to maintain rapid workflow 
An aptitude for learning, organization skills and the ability to follow systems and procedures 
A time-management mindset, along with planning, and prioritization skills
The ability to work independently as well as part of a team 
The ability to express empathy with injured and/or disabled people 
A customer service mindset
Ability to work Monday-Friday on a 1st shift central time zone
Comprehensive benefit package including health/dental/vision insurance, profit sharing, 401k matching, generous time off
Traditional first shift schedule
A relaxed, yet upbeat, work environment, with a jeans professional dress code when onsite
In-office days reserved for training or team events
Awesome downtown location in River North
Close to public transit
Rising was named a Top Workplace in the healthcare industry for 2023! Check out our profile here: Rising Medical Solutions, Inc Profile (topworkplaces.com)

linkedin_org_description:
Rising Medical Solutions is a national managed care firm that provides medical cost containment and medical care management services to the workers'​ compensation, auto, liability, and group health markets. Our client base includes insurance carriers, TPAs, self-insured employers, and public-sector and government entities of varying sizes from small to large tier.

Since 1999, our managed care services have positively impacted hundreds of thousands of employee lives and saved billions in total claim costs for payers across the country. Our flagship bill review service and technologies consistently deliver over 60% in national bill review savings, which includes application of fee schedule/UCR rates, network discounts, nurse audits, provider negotiations, and embedded fraud detection capabilities. 

However, our solutions portfolio goes far beyond the standard repricing of medical bills. Our integrated programs deliver clinical and cost-saving expertise before, during, and after treatment. Our proprietary VISION™ platform is the industry’s only multi-product, multi-line, single-sign-on, medical management enterprise system. VISION™ provides claims professionals with unprecedented ancillary referral and medical management capabilities across the entire medical care continuum, from early intervention to payment processing. Together with our passionate, mission-driven employees, Rising delivers an unparalleled user experience for those providing, receiving, and paying for medical care.

Rising also directs and publishes the annual Workers’ Compensation Benchmarking Study, a national research program examining the complex forces impacting claims management in workers’ compensation today. The study has become one of the most cited research publications in the industry and has been the subject of numerous articles, webinars, and conference presentations since its 2013 inception.

title: Process Development Intern
description_text:

The PD Intern will be supporting the Process Development team with tasks related to product and process development, manufacturing, and quality systems. This internship offers real-world experience in a regulated medical devices environment.
EXPOSURE TO THE FOLLOWING RESPONSIBILITIES
Process Development
Design and optimize manufacturing processes for new or existing medical devices
Document and validate equipment/process changes under ISO and FDA standards

Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

title: Director of Human Resources
description_text:
About Our Client
Regency Supply is a family-owned, national electrical and lighting distributor headquartered in Chatsworth, CA, with over 40 years of experience supporting contractors and maintenance professionals. Formed through the merger of Regency Lighting and AllSale Electric, Regency Supply offers a full suite of solutions, including lighting replacement (MRO), energy retrofits, construction and architectural lighting, design services through RL Studio, and 24/7 online ordering.
With additional locations in Kennesaw, GA, and across Southern California, Regency Supply combines extensive inventory with expert guidance to ensure contractors and clients receive the products and support they need—quickly and reliably. Founded in 1983 by Mike Goldstone and Ron Regenstreif, the company operates on core values of integrity, service, and expertise, often guided by the faith-based principles of its founders.
About the Role
The Director of Human Resources is the senior internal authority on people strategy, risk management, and organizational effectiveness. This role balances hands-on leadership with strategic ownership, acting as a trusted advisor to executive leadership while still operating close to the business.
Key Responsibilities
HR Team Leadership & Organizational Effectiveness
Provide overall leadership and direction for the HR function, including structure, roles, and capability development.
Build and maintain a high-performing HR team through coaching and performance management.
Set priorities and allocate resources to ensure HR efforts align with business objectives.
Establish standards for HR decision-making, service delivery, and risk management.
Mentor HR team members and serve as a role model for judgment, professionalism, and discretion.
Strategic HR Leadership
Translate business strategy into scalable people programs.
Lead workforce planning, succession planning, and organizational design.
Advise ownership on risk, compliance, and people-related financial exposure.
Employee Relations & Risk Management
Own high-risk investigations, executive issues, and sensitive terminations.
Partner directly with legal counsel; determine when escalation is required.
Proactively identify compliance gaps and mitigation strategies.
Compensation, Benefits & Total Rewards
Design, evaluate, and recommend compensation and incentive structures.
Oversee benefits strategy, renewals, funding models, and cost containment.
Provide decision-ready analysis to ownership.
HR Operations & Infrastructure
Oversee payroll, HRIS, leave administration, and vendor relationships.
Ensure processes scale with growth, acquisitions, or restructuring.
Own HR audit readiness and governance.
Workers' Compensation & Risk Management
Provide strategic oversight of the workers’ compensation program, including claims trends, cost drivers, return-to-work strategy, and loss mitigation.
Partner with brokers, carriers, and legal counsel on complex claims, litigation, and settlements.
Advise leadership on risk exposure, experience modification (MOD) factors, and proactive cost-containment strategies.
OSHA & Workplace Safety Compliance
Own OSHA compliance strategy, including policies, training programs, audit readiness, and regulatory response.
Lead investigations of serious incidents and coordinate corrective action plans.
Ensure safety practices scale appropriately across locations and operational environments.
Property & Casualty Insurance Strategy
Serve as the primary internal owner for P&C insurance programs, including workers’ compensation, EPLI, and liability coverage.
Lead annual insurance renewals, broker relationships, and coverage evaluations.
Provide executive-level recommendations on coverage structure, limits, and risk tolerance.
Leadership Development & Culture
Coach leaders on performance, accountability, and behavior alignment.
Champion company values and culture across locations.
Lead training, engagement, and communication strategies. 
Authority & Decision-Making Parameters
Make final recommendations on terminations, investigations, and corrective action.
Select and manage HR vendors within approved budgets.
Redesign HR programs and policies, subject to executive sign-off.
Act as internal decision-maker on compliance and risk matters.
Authorized to manage broker relationships, recommend carriers and coverage structures, and lead renewals within approved financial parameters.
Acts as the company’s internal authority on employment-related risk and insurance strategy, subject to executive sign-off on material financial decisions.
12–18+ years progressive HR leadership; HR certification (SPHR/SHRM-SCP) preferred 
Experience in distribution, warehousing, or operational environments 
Deep California and multi-state labor law expertise 
Comfort operating in gray areas while managing legal and people risk 
Comfort serving as the organization’s internal authority on people-related risk 
Proven executive advisory capability 
Synthesizes people, legal, and financial risk into clear recommendations 
Exceptional judgment in high-risk, high-ambiguity situations 
Proven ability to balance business needs, legal compliance, and employee experience 
Handles confidential information with discretion and restraint 
Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to build trust across a diverse employee population including owners, managers, and frontline employees 
Calm, concise executive presence with ability to maintain composure during conflict, investigations, and executive tension
Medical, Dental, Vision 
Paid Life and AD&D Insurance
Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA) and Health Savings Account (HSA)
Retirement Plans
Paid Time Off
Paid Sick
Paid Holidays 
Employee Discounts
ai_benefits: Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA) and Health Savings Account (HSA)
ai_core_responsibilities: The Director of Human Resources will provide leadership for the HR function, ensuring alignment with business objectives and managing high-risk employee relations issues. This role also involves strategic oversight of compensation, benefits, and compliance with labor laws.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates should have 12–18+ years of progressive HR leadership experience, preferably in distribution or operational environments, along with deep knowledge of California and multi-state labor laws. An HR certification such as SPHR or SHRM-SCP is preferred.

Servant is a digital consultancy that equips faith-driven founders, nonprofits, ministries, and mission-aligned businesses to turn inspired vision into scalable, sustainable impact.

From growth strategy and automation to custom platform development, we help organizations thrive in a digital-first world—expanding reach, reducing friction, and staying focused on what matters most: the mission.

Our Core Services:
➡️ Growth Strategy & Execution
➡️ Automation & Digital Transformation
➡️ Custom Product & Platform Development

Our Global Impact: 
$105M+ raised for Kingdom initiatives 
300M+ lives reached globally 
Solutions delivered in 30+ languages 
Talent from Nike, Google & Amazon—serving the faith-driven sector

At Servant, we build what matters. Whether you’re scaling a global ministry or launching a new initiative, we help you grow with purpose, lead with clarity, and serve with excellence.

title: Advanced Systems Manager Sr - Quantum Sensing Solutions
description_text:
Position Overview
The Advanced Systems Manager is responsible for leading the disciplined capture of new business opportunities within the Quantum Sensing Solutions Business Unit, from BD handoff of qualified opportunities through contract award. This role owns the end-to-end capture process for qualified opportunities, translating early business development efforts into executable program awards.
Operating at the intersection of strategy, customer engagement, proposal leadership, and technical collaboration, the Advanced Systems Manager assembles and leads cross-functional capture teams, develops and executes winning capture strategies, and ensures alignment with Business Unit (BU) leadership and corporate objectives. Upon contract award, responsibility transitions to the QSS Program Execution function, with the Advanced Systems Manager ensuring a clean and informed handoff that has been vetted for execution risk acceptability through a defined gate review process and coordination with the Execution function leadership.
This role is critical to scaling the company’s quantum sensing portfolio across government and commercial customers.
Key Responsibilities
Capture Leadership & Strategy
Assume ownership of qualified opportunities upon transition from the Business Development phase through defined process gates, ensuring clarity of requirements and scope, customer need, competitive landscape, and win strategies and objectives.
Develop and execute capture strategies aligned with Business Unit and corporate growth priorities, including win themes, discriminators, pricing approach, and teaming strategy.
Lead the development of program and technical bid baselines consistent with competitive objectives, established execution risk thresholds, and business unit strategies
In close coordination with Infleqtion Proposal Operations, assigned technical teams, and QSS program management staff, play a critical role in leading development and review of proposal-level integrated master schedules in support to the creation of proposal artifacts, including pricing and cost volumes, technical volumes, schedule graphics, deliverable tables, etc.
Lead all capture gate reviews, preparing materials, driving decision-making, and incorporating leadership guidance into evolving capture plans.
Estimate, manage, and maintain accountability for capture strategy, plans, cost, schedule, and risk throughout the capture process.
Customer & Stakeholder Engagement
Serve as the primary capture interface with government customers, program offices, primes, and key stakeholders during the pre-award phase.
Coordinate closely with Business Development to ensure continuity of customer relationships and messaging.
Define, mature, establish, and maintain strong working relationships with industry teammates, subcontractors, and technology partners. When appropriate and beneficial, lead the development of Teaming Agreements to provide discriminating competitive advantage.
Capture Team Assembly & Leadership
Assemble and lead multi-disciplinary capture teams, including technical leads, proposal managers, pricing/finance, contracts, and supply chain.
Define roles, responsibilities, and expectations for capture team members.
Drive disciplined execution of capture activities, including competitive assessments, solution shaping, and proposal readiness.
Proposal & Pre-Award Execution
Lead capture activities through proposal submission, ensuring technical, management, and cost elements align with a defined and vetted capture strategy.
Working in a highly integrated partnership with Infleqtion Proposal Operations, provide strategic oversight to proposal development, ensuring compliance, clarity, and competitiveness.
Support negotiations and clarifications as required leading up to award, including the development of BAFO phase artifacts as required.
Transition from Business Development
Work in direct partnership with the Infleqtion Business Development team to ensure a seamless transition into the opportunity capture phase:
Participate in the regular cadence of BD opportunity portfolio meetings to ensure a strong working knowledge of the landscape of opportunities being worked by the BD Team in order to efficiently develop capture strategies which recognize the body of work accomplished throughout the BD phase 
Act as a key interface between the BD Team and the BU to lead the planning, provision and organization of BU technical and/or program management staff required to support the effective maturation of opportunities through the qualification process
As required, support the BD team in the establishment and codification of forecast information for opportunities in the BD phase as required to support QSS resource planning needs and the management of the QSS annual operating plan 
Transition to Execution
Lead the transition from capture to execution upon contract award, ensuring:
Adequate review and vetting of proposal program baselines by the QSS Program Execution/management staff as needed to ensure an acceptable and aligned balance of competitive positioning vs. execution risk
Clear communication of customer expectations and commitments
Transfer of capture knowledge, assumptions, and risks
Required Qualifications
Bachelor’s degree in engineering, physics, business, or a related field; advanced degree preferred.
8+ years of experience in aerospace, defense, or national security environments, with direct experience in capture management or closely related roles.
Demonstrated success leading captures resulting in contract awards, including complex technical programs.
Strong understanding of U.S. government acquisition processes, contracting vehicles, and proposal development best practices.
Experience working with advanced technologies (e.g., sensing, photonics, quantum, RF, or similar deep-tech domains).
Excellent leadership, communication, and cross-functional collaboration skills.
U.S. citizenship and ability to obtain and maintain a security clearance.
Preferred Qualifications
Direct experience with quantum sensing, precision navigation, advanced ISR, or emerging defense technologies.
Experience working in a small or mid-sized defense firm, with comfort operating in a fast-paced, resource-constrained environment.
Familiarity with OTA, SBIR/STTR, and other non-traditional acquisition mechanisms.
Experience in formulating, baselining, and execution of developmental and recurring effort Government Program contracts. 
Existing relationships within DoD, IC, or aerospace primes relevant to quantum sensing applications.
Success Metrics
Quality and consistency of capture execution across qualified opportunities
Win rate and strategic alignment of awarded contracts
Effectiveness of capture team leadership and gate review outcomes
Smooth and effective transition from BD to capture and capture to execution
Salary range: $140,000 to $152,000
100% company-paid medical, dental, vision, short/long-term disability 
Employer-funded Health Savings Account 
Unlimited PTO 
401(k) match 
Company-paid Life and AD&D Insurance 
Flexible Savings Account 
Paid FMLA, Maternity/Paternity Leave 
Employee Assistance Program 
Student Loan Repayment 
Equity Program

This role involves developing capture strategies, assembling cross-functional teams, and managing customer engagement throughout the proposal process.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must have a bachelor's degree in a relevant field and at least 8 years of experience in aerospace, defense, or national security environments. A strong understanding of U.S. government acquisition processes and experience with advanced technologies are also required.

title: Speech Language Pathologist
description_text:
Our employees and our patients are the foundation of our success, and their dedication is what truly makes Amazing Care… AMAZING! 
Founded in 2004, Amazing Care Home Health Services was built on a simple but powerful belief: when employees are supported, empowered, and valued, exceptional patient care follows. For over two decades, we’ve remained committed to creating an environment where compassion, accountability, and quality are not just values—but daily standards. 
We provide high-quality, in-home care to individuals and families, partnering closely with clinicians, caregivers, and operational teams to ensure patients receive timely, personalized support. At Amazing Care, we lead with integrity, collaboration, and a strong sense of ownership in everything we do. 
We are proud to work alongside professionals who live our ARTIE values — Accountability, Recognition, Teamwork, Integrity and Excellence every day. 
We are seeking a Speech Language Pathologist to join our team in the Ogden area. A speech-language pathologist (SLP) will implement treatment plans to assist clients of all ages with cognitive, speech, language, feeding/swallowing, and/or social/emotional functioning by developing and administering speech therapy services in the home and community settings.
Provides compassionate and high-quality care that meets the needs of the client and family by performing evaluations and interpreting assessment results, developing a plan of care including skilled interventions and goals appropriate to the client's needs, and creating and implementing speech and language treatment plans in conjunction with the physician.
Assists pediatric and adult patients to develop or regain cognitive, speech, language, feeding/swallowing, and/or social/emotional functioning and improves their level of independence and quality of life.
Coordinates care with referring physician and other health care organizations and personnel to ensure safe, appropriate, and effective care for the client.
Instructs client, family/caregiver, and other organization health care personnel in the client's treatment plan as indicated.
Identifies client and family/caregiver needs for other services and refers as appropriate.
Prepares and submits documentation per organization policy.
Maintains clinical competency in the theory and practice of speech-language pathology.
Pay: $65-$70 per visit (visit duration varies from 30-90 minutes)
Mileage provided for commuting between patient visits
Schedule:
Part-Time Days ,10-20 visits per week
A master's or doctoral degree in speech-language pathology (SLP)
ASHA Certification or documentation of passing PRAXIS exam and completion of a Clinical Fellow/Externship
Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP) License in the state of Utah
Active BLS certification 
Valid driver's license and auto insurance
A minimum of 1-year clinical experience in Speech Language Pathology is preferred, but not required
Comprehensive Health Coverage: Medical, dental, and vision insurance options to support your overall health and well-being. 
Financial Protection: Life, disability, pet, and legal insurance options for added peace of mind. 
Paid Time Off: Generous paid time off to support work-life balance, rest, and personal needs. 
Weekly Pay: Enjoy the convenience and consistency of weekly pay. 
Supportive Work Environment: Benefits are part of a broader commitment to employee well-being within a stable and growing organization. 
Why Join Us: 
Make a real impact: Be part of a mission-driven organization where your work directly supports individuals and families receiving critical in-home care. Your role plays a key part in ensuring clients are connected to services quickly and accurately. 
Collaborative, people-first culture: Join a supportive, team-oriented environment that values accountability, communication, and shared success. You’ll work closely with clinical and operational leaders who are invested in doing things the right way. 
Growth and development: Amazing Care is committed to developing talent from within. You’ll have opportunities to expand your skills, take on increased responsibility, and grow your career as the organization continues to scale. 
Competitive compensation and benefits: We offer a competitive salary, comprehensive benefits, and a stable work environment within a growing healthcare organization.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Speech Language Pathologist will implement treatment plans to assist clients of all ages with cognitive, speech, language, feeding/swallowing, and/or social/emotional functioning by developing and administering speech therapy services in home and community settings. This includes performing evaluations, developing plans of care, and coordinating care with physicians and other healthcare personnel.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess a master's or doctoral degree in speech-language pathology, ASHA Certification or documentation of passing the PRAXIS exam and completing a Clinical Fellow/Externship, and a Speech-Language Pathologist License in Utah. A valid driver's license, auto insurance, and active BLS certification are also required, with a minimum of 1 year of clinical experience preferred but not mandatory.

title: Email Copywriter for US DTC Agency (Remote)
description_text:
Paired is a global staffing and recruiting agency that specializes in pairing remote work with top-tier talent!
We are dedicated to connecting talented individuals with remarkable companies in need of their skills. Our goal is to create fulfilling job opportunities for professionals around the world.

We’re looking for a skilled Email Copywriter with hands-on experience writing high-performing email campaigns for DTC brands or agencies, preferably serving the US market. This role is ideal for someone who understands how email drives revenue in eCommerce — from promotions and launches to retention and lifecycle flows.
You’ll be responsible for crafting clear, persuasive, and on-brand email copy that converts, while working closely with marketing and creative teams to support growth initiatives.
Key Responsibilities
Write engaging, conversion-focused email copy for DTC eCommerce brands
Develop campaigns for promotions, product launches, newsletters, and announcements
Create and optimize lifecycle flows (welcome, abandoned cart, post-purchase, win-back, etc.)
Collaborate with designers and marketers to align copy with visuals and strategy
Adapt tone and messaging based on brand voice and audience segments
Review performance data and iterate on copy to improve open rates, CTR, and revenue
Maintain consistency and quality across all email communications
Proven experience as an Email Copywriter for DTC brands or marketing agencies 
Strong understanding of eCommerce email strategy and customer lifecycle marketing 
Excellent written English (native or near-native level preferred) 
Experience writing for US-based audiences is a strong plus 
Ability to work full-time, aligned with US working hours 
Comfortable working remotely and managing deadlines independently 
Portfolio of email campaigns or flows that demonstrate performance-driven copy
Nice to Have
Experience working with ESPs such as Klaviyo, HubSpot, or similar
Familiarity with A/B testing and performance optimization
Agency background supporting multiple brands
Fully remote, full-time contractor role 
Competitive compensation up to $2,500 USD/month 
Opportunity to work with fast-growing DTC brands 
Clear expectations, autonomy, and long-term collaboration potential
ai_core_responsibilities: The primary responsibility is crafting clear, persuasive, and on-brand email copy that drives conversions for DTC eCommerce brands across various campaign types, including promotions and lifecycle flows. This involves collaborating with marketing and creative teams to align copy with visuals and strategy while maintaining quality across all communications.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must possess proven experience as an Email Copywriter, ideally for DTC brands or marketing agencies, with a strong understanding of eCommerce email strategy and customer lifecycle marketing. Essential requirements include excellent written English, the ability to work full-time aligned with US working hours, and comfort managing deadlines independently in a remote setting.

Paired is a leading headhunting agency that specializes in sourcing and recruiting top creative and design talent from overseas. We pride ourselves on our ability to identify and attract the very best candidates for our clients.

At Paired, we understand that hiring the right talent is critical to the success of any organization. That's why we take a personalized, consultative approach to the recruitment, working closely with our clients to understand their unique needs and requirements, and tailoring our search and selection process accordingly.

title: Portfolio Operations Specialist (Trade Support experience)
description_text:
Portfolio Operations Specialist
Locations: Mexico & Argentina Work Modality: Contractor – Onsite
Schedule: Availability required between 6:00 AM – 7:00 PM (EST)
Role Overview: The Portfolio Operations Specialist will execute core operational processes related to financial transactions, reconciliations, and investment portfolio support. The role demands strong attention to detail, analytical skills, solid knowledge of financial products, and effective communication with internal and external stakeholders.
Key Responsibilities:
• Execute daily operational activities including Trade Matching, Settlements, Post-Settlements, and Corporate Actions.
• Reconcile portfolio positions and cash balances between internal systems and custodians or fund administrators.
• Investigate, analyze, and resolve breaks or discrepancies accurately and promptly.
• Maintain data integrity and accuracy across portfolio management systems, including configurations, corporate events, and pricing.
• Collaborate closely with custodians and external counterparties to support investment operations through clear and effective communication.
• Perform ad-hoc tasks as requested by clients, senior team members, or team leads.
• Continuously develop knowledge of multiple financial instruments including equities, fixed income, swaps, options, FX, mortgages, financing, and futures.
Requirements & Qualifications:
Education:
• Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Accounting, or related field.
Professional Experience:
• 3 to 5 years experience in investment operations, trade support, or portfolio reconciliation within multinational banks, investment firms, hedge funds, or asset managers.
• Strong understanding of the trade lifecycle, asset classes, and reconciliation processes.
Technical Skills:
• Solid knowledge of financial products, especially derivatives, including Futures, Swaps, Options, FX, OTC instruments.
• Familiarity with Global Equities and Fixed Income.
• Advanced English proficiency (B2+/C1 level - written and spoken).
• Advanced Excel skills capable of analyzing complex financial data.
• Experience with OMS/PMS platforms such as Enfusion, Eze Software/Eclipse, Geneva, Portfolio One (Layer One), Sage Intacct is highly valued.
Soft Skills:
• Exceptional attention to detail and accuracy in data handling.
• Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.
• Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced operational environment.

#Solvo

\n

\n
$2,300 - $2,350 a month
\n

They must investigate and resolve discrepancies promptly and maintain data integrity across portfolio management systems.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates need 3 to 5 years of experience in investment operations, trade support, or portfolio reconciliation, requiring a Bachelor's degree in Finance, Economics, or Accounting. Strong technical knowledge of financial products, especially derivatives, and advanced English proficiency are mandatory.

title: Account Manager - SAFE Transportation
description_text:
Who are we?
SAFE Transportation (Powered by Dynamic Connections) is a premier, high-touch freight brokerage that provides intelligent, reliable logistics solutions for customers ranging from Fortune 10 companies to small, privately owned manufacturers across North America. Every shipment we manage is handled with precision, clear communication, and accountability --delivering peace of mind and a seamless transportation experience. In an industry where the cost of failure is high, we’ve built our reputation by doing things the right way and following through every step of the way.

At SAFE, we treat our employees the way we treat our customers -- with honesty, integrity, and respect. We operate as collaborative teams where everyone contributes, supports one another, and takes ownership of their work. While some tasks are handled individually, every role is part of the bigger picture, and success comes from working together. Through our partnership with Dynamic Connections, we combine our personalized, high-touch service with leading technology, resources, and training to support our people and customers alike. We have a positive, transparent, and team-oriented culture, and we provide meaningful opportunities for growth to those who are accountable, driven, and ready to build a long-term career.
\n

Who We’re Looking For:
At SAFE, we manage complex, high-stakes shipments while making logistics seamless for our customers. As an Account Manager, you’ll be at the center of creating and executing solutions that provide reliability, peace of mind, and exceptional service.
You’ll join a tight-knit operations team that works collaboratively to support our diverse portfolio of customers. Day-to-day, you’ll be responsible for tracking shipments, pricing and quoting, dispatching carrier partners, and ensuring a best-in-class customer experience. Transportation moves fast, priorities can shift quickly, and every shipment matters -- so we look for people who thrive under pressure, take ownership, and enjoy solving problems in real time.
If you are accountable, hardworking, and a team player who values both customer success and collaboration, SAFE offers a challenging, fast-paced, and rewarding environment where you can build a meaningful career -- not just take a job.

What You Will Bring to This Role:
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, time-sensitive environment
Tenacity and persistence; you see challenges through to successful outcomes
Strong judgment and problem-solving skills
Experience in a customer-facing role with a clear focus on delivering value
Exceptional communication skills; you build trust, foster relationships, and ensure customer needs are met
Strong computer skills and proficiency with Microsoft Office

To Be Successful in This Role, You Are:
Exceptionally detail-oriented. You catch errors before they become problems and take pride in getting things right the first time.
High energy and focused. SAFE is a fast-paced, high-accountability environment, and you thrive under pressure and deadlines.
Self-motivated. You take ownership of your work and consistently perform at your best without needing constant oversight.
Highly collaborative. You actively support your teammates and work together to ensure success for customers and the team.
A relationship-builder. You care deeply about your customers and carriers, treating their business as if it were your own and fostering long-term partnerships.

Our pledge to you and what we offer: 
This is a full-time, permanent position working with SAFE’s close-knit, high-performing team. You’ll be part of a collaborative environment where every contribution matters.
Competitive compensation and benefits, including tools and technology to help you do your best work.
Performance-based incentives where results are recognized quickly, and clear opportunities for advancement for high performers.
Real professional development. SAFE invests in your growth, offering training, mentorship, and opportunities to expand your skills and career.
A culture rooted in integrity, teamwork, and accountability. We celebrate successes together, host team events, and recognize contributions in meaningful ways.

\n
$55,000 - $60,000 a year
\n
SAFE is committed to building a diverse, inclusive, and supportive workplace. We value people who are accountable, collaborative, and driven to make a difference -- people who want to build a long-term career, not just a job.
ai_core_responsibilities: The Account Manager will be responsible for tracking shipments, pricing and quoting services, dispatching carrier partners, and ensuring a best-in-class customer experience for complex, high-stakes shipments. This role requires creating and executing logistics solutions that provide reliability and peace of mind to a diverse portfolio of customers.
ai_requirements_summary: Candidates must thrive in a fast-paced, time-sensitive environment, demonstrating tenacity, strong judgment, and real-time problem-solving skills while managing high-stakes logistics. Essential qualifications include exceptional communication skills for building trust, strong computer proficiency, and a highly detail-oriented, self-motivated approach to work.
linkedin_org_slogan: Your Partner to Success
linkedin_org_industry: Transportation, Logistics, Supply Chain and Storage
linkedin_org_locations: 3270 South Service Road, Unit 5, Oakville, Oakville L6L 0B1, CA
5150 Pacific Coast Highway, Suite 200, Long Beach, California 90804, US
linkedin_org_description:
Dynamic Connections offers completely integrated logistic solutions to meet our customer’s individual needs while maintaining a high level of consistency through communication and customer service. Our solutions may change, but our disciplined Customer Success Cycle (CSC) operational process remains the same for every order. Our consistent service and communication gives you the peace of mind to understand what you are getting each time, on every shipment. We strive to be an extension of your company with the goal to assist in your profitable growth by building a bridge of success between all parties through Competent, Confident, Communication. We understand that each shipment is a step in the profit chain of both your company and your customer. We support the chain and its future with our CSC on every opportunity. We will start as a supplier and grow to become your “Partner to Success” for the future.

Please give us the opportunity to provide you with a free quote.  Please email us at solutions@dynamicconnections.com 24/7/365.

title: Physical Therapist Assistant – Choose Your Schedule
description_text:
At MedCare Pediatric, we’re committed to transforming lives through compassionate, patient-centered care. As a trusted provider of home health services, we bring therapy directly into homes, daycares, and schools—where patients feel safest and most supported. Our mission is to empower individuals of all ages to reach their fullest potential by delivering exceptional therapeutic services grounded in integrity, collaboration, and excellence.

As a Home Health Physical Therapy Assistant, you’ll provide personalized, one-on-one care in the environments where patients thrive. Our structured scheduling allows for meaningful interactions, focused treatment sessions, and detailed documentation—while offering the flexibility to support your work-life balance. You’ll join a collaborative, purpose-driven team that values professional development and equips you with the tools to make a lasting difference in the lives of the children and families we serve.

Are you ready to make a difference where it matters most? Apply today and become part of a team committed to compassionate, in-home care!
\n

Physical Therapy Assistant (PTA) Qualifications & Responsibilities:
Graduate from an accredited school program for Physical Therapy Assistant
Texas Physical Therapy Assistant License (or able to option before the anticipated start date)
1 year of experience preferred, but new graduates are welcome to apply
Bilingual in Spanish is a plus
Provides physical therapy services, including range of motion, strength, mobility, and caregiver training, following physician-approved plans.
Educates families on physical therapy techniques and collaborates with team members for comprehensive care.
Administers therapy to improve functional abilities and independence
Work closely with interdisciplinary teams to ensure quality care

Sign-On Bonus

Support from our team of dedicated clinical supervisors and administrative staff.

Flexible Shifts and Scheduling to Fit Your Lifestyle

Transform Lives as a Home Health Physical Therapy Assistant at MedCare Pediatric

description_text:
At MedCare Pediatric, we are committed to delivering personalized, one-on-one care that empowers children to communicate, connect, and grow. Our mission is centered on understanding each child’s unique needs and partnering with families to create therapy plans that foster meaningful progress. By supporting our therapists with the tools and time they need, we ensure that the focus remains on creating lasting impacts for every child and family we serve.


As a Clinical Fellow – Speech-Language Pathologist (CF-SLP) at MedCare, you’ll have the opportunity to transform the lives of children and their families. By addressing critical needs in speech, language, feeding, and swallowing, you play a vital role in helping children overcome challenges and develop essential skills. Collaboration and continuous growth are central to our approach, and you’ll work alongside a team of professionals equally committed to making a difference.

title: RevOps Manager
description_text:
Join Modulate, a frontier AI lab building some of the world’s best voice understanding models. 

At Modulate, we’re building a new generation of voice AI to help people and platforms better understand and navigate real human conversation. Our proprietary voice understanding model captures nuance, intent, and emotion in real time, enabling safer, more responsible, and more effective voice-driven experiences. 
We’ve already protected hundreds of millions of people from harassment, fraud, manipulation, and more through our work with top gaming studios and Fortune 500 companies — and we’re just getting started. 

We’re looking for a Revenue Operations Manager to strengthen and scale the systems, processes, and data infrastructure that power Modulate’s go-to-market engine. Reporting to the Director of BizOps & Strategy, this is a high-impact role that sits at the center of Sales, Marketing, Customer Success, and Strategy. 

You’ll own the tools, data, and workflows that ensure our teams operate with clarity, consistency, and precision. If you love making messy processes smooth, making data trustworthy, and building foundations that unlock predictable growth, this could be the perfect role for you.

Your Impact
As our Revenue Operations Manager, your work will directly shape how Modulate grows, and how effectively we bring our mission-driven technology to the world. 

- Leadership makes better, faster decisions. You’ll improve forecasting accuracy, pipeline visibility, and metric clarity so executives and GTM leaders can confidently plan, prioritize, and invest for long-term growth.
- Revenue teams operate with focus and momentum. By designing clean, scalable processes and automations, you’ll reduce friction and manual work, enabling Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success to spend more time closing, retaining, and expanding customers.
- Data becomes a competitive advantage. You’ll turn fragmented or inconsistent data into a trusted source of truth that reveals what’s working, where we’re losing momentum, and how to improve conversion, velocity, and retention.
- Modulate scales responsibly and sustainably. By building strong operational foundations early, you’ll help ensure that as we grow, we do so with rigor, transparency, and systems that support both performance and people.

What You’ll Do
- Drive operational excellence across the revenue funnel: Work with BD directors to scope out, document, and refine end-to-end GTM processes, from lead routing to opportunity management to cross-team handoffs; build scalable, repeatable workflows that reduce friction and improve execution quality for Sales, Marketing, and CS. 
- Build dashboards, reporting, and forecasting foundations: Develop and maintain dashboards and automated reports that provide leadership and operating teams with appropriate visibility into pipeline health, conversion rates, retention, and other core GTM metrics.
- Unlock automation using AI and workflow tools to reduce manual work across teams: Build and optimize end-to-end automations across the GTM tech stack to remove bottlenecks, improve data consistency, and ensure teams can operate at scale; regularly audit workflows, identify gaps, and introduce system-level improvements that reduce operational overhead and enable smoother execution. 
- Run data-driven improvements & experimentation: Debug funnel issues (e.g., drop-off points, inconsistent behaviors, missing data); propose and test process changes or system configurations that meaningfully improve conversion, velocity, or data fidelity; ensure GTM leaders have the information they need to make confident, timely decisions.
- Facilitate consistent trainingand serve as subject matter expert: Become fluent in our ICP, tech stack, and processes, such that any member of our BD team can come to you for help and training in all of the above. 
- Own the revenue systems ecosystem: Administer and optimize key GTM tools including HubSpot, Default, Notion, LeadIQ, Amplemarket, Swan, and related integrations; ensure accurate data capture, clean CRM hygiene, and consistent use of fields, stages, and workflows; serve as the go-to resource for troubleshooting, configuration, and long-term system improvements.

WhoYouAre
- 3–5+ years of experience in Revenue Operations, Sales Ops, Marketing Ops, BizOps, or a related function at a B2B SaaS or AI company.
- Strong proficiency with HubSpot CRM, Default, Notion, and preferably other workflow or automation tools.
- Highly analytical - able to dig into data, clean it, structure it, and surface insights that drive action.
- Exceptionally detail-oriented and process-driven; you intuitively notice inefficiencies and enjoy fixing them.
- Comfortable balancing strategic thinking with hands-on technical execution.
- Clear, thoughtful communicator who collaborates exceptionally well across teams.
- Energized by shaping early systems in a growing org - and maintaining them as they scale.
- Passionate about AI, ethical innovation, and Modulate’s mission to make online voice communication safer.
- Bachelor’s degree in Business or equivalent experience.

Benefits
At Modulate, we prioritize people and sustainability—because doing great work requires feeling supported and safe. Some of our key benefits include:
- Competitive salary + equity- Full health, dental, and vision coverage
- Flexible PTO, with a strong culture of taking it
- Weekly team lunches with dietary accommodations
- Hybrid work: core in-office days with flexible remote options
- Regular leadership and industry learning sessions
- Support for career development and continued learning
- Up to 8 weeks work-from-anywhere policy
- A deeply inclusive, human-centered culture

Pay Transparency
Modulate believes in transparency as a cornerstone of equity and trust. Compensation for this role is based on seniority, skills, and experience.
Salary:$120K–$140K
Equity: Offered
Other perks: HSA, FSA, 15 holidays, professional growth resources

About Modulate
Modulate is on a mission to make voice a force for good online. Our tools help communities thrive by proactively detecting toxic behavior, protecting user identity, and empowering safety teams. We’re trusted by leaders in gaming and beyond—and we’re growing fast. 
We believe that great cultures don’t just happen. That’s why we’ve built a foundation of intentional systems: from bias-reducing hiring practices to transparent pay to tools that help teams collaborate across communication styles. At Modulate, we treat people like people—and we’re building technology that does the same.

Ready to join us? Apply here or reach out directly—we’re excited to meet you.

A quick note as you apply
We kindly ask that you apply through the website rather than emailing careers@modulate.ai. 
For the questions “Your fit for the role,” “Your values/goals,” and “Why Modulate?” on our application form, we kindly ask that you focus your responses on experiences and motivations that directly relate to the role. Please avoid including details that would reveal protected demographic information (such as race, age, gender, ethnicity, or sexual orientation), as we strive to assess all candidates equitably and without bias.
We also want to hear your authentic voice. While it can be helpful to use tools to support your thinking, we ask that your responses reflect your own perspective. Applications with significant overlap or duplication from other submissions will not be considered.
\n

\n
$120,000 - $140,000 a year
\n

Responsibilities also include unlocking automation using AI and workflow tools to reduce manual work and serving as the subject matter expert for the revenue systems ecosystem.

The ideal person is detail-oriented, comfortable balancing strategy with hands-on execution, and passionate about AI and Modulate's mission.

Modulate is the top expert in voice analysis -- from gaming communities to insurance, finance, delivery, and healthcare.

We power two flagship platforms:

ToxMod, offering proactive voice-chat moderation to reduce toxicity and harassment in multiplayer environments.

VoiceVault, providing real-time fraud detection across voice channels, including contact centers and phone-based claim lines.

Our solutions analyze tone, intent, emotion, speaker/listener behavior, and conversational context—scalable to millions of interactions in 18 languages. We help enterprises mitigate risk, support agents, and improve customer outcomes—delivering security and trust everywhere your voice matters.

title: Customer Success Manager (Founding Team)
description_text:
Scality builds the world’s most trusted software-defined storage systems — powering banks, service providers, media companies, and large enterprises across the globe. Our solutions deliver data to hundreds of millions of users every month with 100% availability.

As we grow in 2026, we are evolving our partnership model. We are known for our deep technical excellence; now, we are adding a dedicated Customer Success layer to ensure our clients are getting the maximum business value out of their investment. We are looking for a CSM who wants to help us build this philosophy and act as the strategic bridge between our technology and our customers' long-term goals.

In this role, you will be the advocate for the customer’s business health.
\n

What You’ll Do
Strategic Partnership: Manage a portfolio of enterprise accounts, acting as their primary business point of contact post-deployment.
Business Reviews: Lead quarterly reviews to track adoption, discuss product roadmaps, and align Scality’s capabilities with the customer’s evolving data strategy.
Build the Playbook: As an early member of the CSM team, you will help us establish our "Success Philosophy": defining the templates, health scores, and engagement strategies we use to measure customer satisfaction.
Mobilize Resources: When a customer has a new use case, needs a new feature or a deep-dive session, you’ll partner with our Sales, Product and Technical Services teams to make it happen.
Voice of the Customer: Take the real-world feedback you hear from clients and funnel it back to our Product teams to help shape what we build next.

Your First 90 Days: We don't expect you to change the world on day one. Your first month will be spent embedding with our Technical Services team to learn the "Scality Way." By day 60, you will be leading your first strategic business reviews, and by day 90, you will be helping us finalize the framework for our 2026 Customer Success philosophy.

Who You Are
The Relationship Lead: You have 5+ years of experience in Customer Success or Account Management, ideally in the enterprise infrastructure or software space, with a focus on driving long-term retention and account health.
Technically Conversant: You do not need to be a storage engineer, but you should be comfortable discussing high-level concepts like cloud storage, data protection, and hybrid-cloud environments.
A Pragmatic Builder: You enjoy the process of formalizing a role and helping an organization mature its customer engagement strategy.
Clear & Direct: You can take complex technical updates and summarize them into clear, actionable business insights for a customer's leadership team.

\n
At Scality, you will join a global, multicultural team that has spent years perfecting the art of software-defined storage. We’ve already built a world-class Technical Services organization that handles the heavy lifting of support and service delivery; your role is to build the strategic layer that sits on top of that foundation. This is a unique opportunity to help transition an established technical leader into a proactive, success-driven partner for our clients.

We value autonomy, direct communication, and a "builder" mindset. If you are looking for a role where you can help define a success philosophy from the ground up—while supported by some of the best engineers in the industry—we would love to have you on board to help us shape the future of our customer relationships.
ai_core_responsibilities: The manager will strategically manage a portfolio of enterprise accounts post-deployment, acting as the primary business contact and leading quarterly reviews to track adoption and align Scality's capabilities with customer goals. title: Researcher I (Contract)
description_text:
At WHOOP, we're on a mission to unlock human performance. WHOOP empowers its members to perform at a higher level through a deeper understanding of their bodies and daily lives. WHOOP Labs is the research and hardware validation arm of WHOOP. Our work focuses on conducting human trials to validate next-gen features and technology.

As a Researcher I, you will join the WHOOP Labs team to execute data collections for a range of research studies for algorithm training and design testing. Your efforts will support the organization as we continue to develop a best in class experience and lead the wearables industry.

This is a W-2, Temporary role through the remainder of 2026, with an hourly pay range of $28 - $32. Candidates must be prepared to work in person at WHOOP Labs in Boston, MA 5 days each week. 
\n

RESPONSIBILITIES: 
Work collaboratively with other researchers to execute testing protocols
Provide coaching and guidance to participants during exercise protocols, balancing data integrity, research best practices, and participant experience
Create a supportive and welcome environment for participants engaging in a range of research tasks, from exercises to device interactions
Manage the use and upkeep of data collection equipment and prototype devices
Report research progress regularly and highlight data quality issues or other bugs to relevant technical teams
Own daily lab management tasks required for data collections to be possible, such as laundry, equipment cleaning, and material organization
Serve as an ambassador for the WHOOP Labs and WHOOP brand

QUALIFICATIONS:
Bachelor's Degree in human physiology, biomedical engineering, exercise physiology, or similar
Strong technological background and working knowledge of data management systems including the Google Suite
Strong interpersonal communication skills with a savvy for working as part of a highly collaborative, fast-paced team
Prior experience in customer service or other public-facing roles a plus 
Strong attention to detail
Professional, timely, organized

\n
Interested in the role but don’t meet every qualification? We encourage you to apply! At WHOOP, we value potential, character, and curiosity as much as experience.

WHOOP is an Equal Opportunity Employer and participates in E-Verify to determine employment eligibility. It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.

title: Lab Operations Coordinator, Front of House (Contract)
description_text:
At WHOOP, we’re on a mission to unlock human performance and healthspan. WHOOP empowers members to perform at a higher level through a deeper understanding of their bodies and daily lives.

WHOOP Labs is where participants experience WHOOP in person, and the Front of House Contractor plays a key role in shaping that experience. We’re looking for someone who brings genuine hospitality, positive energy, and exceptional people skills to create a welcoming, comfortable, and memorable environment for every participant. This role is ideal for someone who loves hosting, connecting with people, and being the friendly face that sets the tone for the day.

This is a W-2, Temporary role through the remainder of 2026, with an hourly pay range of $28 - $32. Candidates must be prepared to work in person at WHOOP Labs in Boston, MA 5 days each week. 
\n

RESPONSIBILITIES: 
Serve as the first point of contact for all WHOOP Labs participants, providing a friendly, professional, and welcoming check-in experience
Clearly explain WHOOP Labs policies, study expectations, and available amenities to participants upon arrival
Act as a WHOOP brand ambassador, sharing what WHOOP is and why it matters while creating an engaging and trustworthy participant experience
Communicate with participants ahead of their visits via messaging platforms to provide directions, building access instructions, and arrival guidance
Coordinate participant flow across studies, ensuring Research Coordinators know who has arrived and are ready to begin sessions on time
Create a fun, comfortable, and inviting lab environment that encourages participants to return for future studies
Maintain a clean, organized, and welcoming space throughout the lab, including waiting areas, restrooms, and shared spaces
Support Research team with operational needs, including laundry, room resets, and general lab upkeep
Monitor and restock towels, snacks, beverages, and other participant amenities as needed
Anticipate participant needs and proactively address questions or concerns

QUALIFICATIONS:
2+ years of experience in hospitality, customer service, front desk, retail, or guest facing roles preferred
Strong interpersonal and communication skills, comfortable engaging with a wide range of participants
Strong attention to detail with the ability to manage multiple participants and schedules at once
Comfortable coordinating with multiple teams and providing real time updates
Reliable, proactive, and able to take initiative in maintaining a high-quality environment
Passion for health, wellness, and delivering exceptional guest experiences
Ability to perform light physical tasks such as laundry, restocking supplies, and maintaining shared spaces
A baseline level of technical comfort, including the ability to answer general WHOOP product and membership questions and guide participants to the right resources when needed.

SHIFT EXPECTATIONS:
This role follows a consistent schedule of Tuesday through Saturday, 7:00 AM - 3:00 PM
Punctuality and reliability are essential, as this role supports participant arrivals and study schedule
Ability to remain engaged, energetic, and guest-focused throughout the shift

\n
Interested in the role but don’t meet every qualification? We encourage you to apply! At WHOOP, we value potential, character, and curiosity as much as experience.

WHOOP is an Equal Opportunity Employer and participates in E-Verify to determine employment eligibility. It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.

title: Distributed & Quantitative Research Specialist (Contract)
description_text:
At WHOOP, we’re on a mission to unlock human performance. WHOOP empowers members to optimize their performance through a deeper understanding of their bodies and daily lives. WHOOP Labs is the central research organization at WHOOP, supporting both in-person and distributed research across product, R&D, and clinical initiatives.

As the Distributed and Quantitative Research Specialist, you will be a key contributor within WHOOP Labs, supporting the growing demand for surveys, intervention studies, and other remote research initiatives through Digital WHOOP Labs (DWL). This role sits at the intersection of research execution and research operations, with a strong focus on enabling high-quality, scalable studies for stakeholders across WHOOP.

This position reports to a Senior Human Factors Manager and is ideal for someone who enjoys hands-on research execution, stakeholder collaboration, and building rigor into large-scale distributed studies.

This is a W-2, Temporary role through the remainder of 2026, with an hourly pay range of $34 - $36. Candidates must be prepared to work in person at WHOOP Labs in Boston, MA 5 days each week. 
\n

RESPONSIBILITIES: 
Learn and maintain a deep understanding of the current and future capabilities of Digital WHOOP Labs (DWL) and other survey and research platforms available to WHOOP Labs
Serve as a primary point of contact for incoming distributed research requests, meeting with stakeholders to understand research goals and translate them into effective study designs
Support design of research protocols that align appropriate remote research methodologies to learning goals to meet stakeholder needs
Partner closely with senior researchers to refine research scopes and ensure studies are feasible, rigorous, and aligned with best practices
Own the executional details of distributed research, including survey review, backend study setup, and facilitation of live and ongoing studies
Review stakeholder-created surveys for quality, clarity, and adherence to quantitative research and survey design best practices
Monitor active studies, troubleshoot participant issues, and communicate bugs or system limitations to internal tooling and technical teams
Interact directly with WHOOP members via Salesforce to resolve issues related to distributed studies and device connectivity
Support operational workflows that enable DWL to scale as a core quantitative research platform across WHOOP
Regularly report study progress, risks, and executional challenges to the broader WHOOP Labs team

QUALIFICATIONS: 
Bachelor’s degree in human physiology, exercise physiology, psychology, biotechnology, human factors engineering, or a related field
2–3 years of experience in quantitative research, product research, or a related research-focused role
Strong foundation in survey design and quantitative research methods, including consumer-grade and mixed-methods approaches
Experience with cross-functional stakeholder management and translating research needs into executable study designs
Familiarity with distributed or remote research platforms; clinical research experience is a plus
UX research experience is a nice-to-have, but not required
Strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and ability to manage multiple concurrent studies
Comfortable working with internal tools and member-facing platforms such as Salesforce

\n
Interested in the role but don’t meet every qualification? We encourage you to apply! At WHOOP, we value potential, character, and curiosity as much as experience.

WHOOP is an Equal Opportunity Employer and participates in E-Verify to determine employment eligibility. It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment.
